key_insight: Two-Sided L-BFGS adds a geometric safeguard that keeps the inverse Hessian condition number uniformly bounded while preserving the standard L-BFGS memory and computational benefits.
engineering_relevance: This makes large-scale optimization more numerically stable and robust on ill-conditioned problems without sacrificing the efficiency needed in engineering-scale applications.
method: Use a two-sided geometric envelope to constrain the extreme eigenvalues of the L-BFGS inverse Hessian approximation.
Track the evolution of eigenvalues across m quasi-Newton updates from a scaled identity start to derive an explicit condition-number bound.
Preserve curvature accumulation and standard O(mn) memory/per-iteration complexity under the safeguarded update rule.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on high-dimensional ill-conditioned benchmarks showing better-conditioned inverse Hessian approximations and improved robustness/convergence.
watch_item: The safeguard adds hyperparameters and line-search assumptions, so practical tuning and behavior outside the stated smooth/strong-Wolfe setting may require care.
paper_title: On the Condition Number Upper Bound of the L-BFGS Inverse Hessian Approximation Matrix with a Two-Sided Geometric Envelope Safeguarding Mechanism
